constellations.name: Andromeda
constellations.description: The Chained Princess. Contains the Andromeda Galaxy (M31), the nearest major galaxy to the Milky Way.

constellations.name: Antlia
constellations.description: The Air Pump. A faint constellation introduced in the 18th century.

constellations.name: Apus
constellations.description: The Bird of Paradise. Represents the exotic birds found in New Guinea.

constellations.name: Aquarius
constellations.description: The Water Bearer. One of the zodiac constellations, often depicted as a man pouring water from a jar.

constellations.name: Aquila
constellations.description: The Eagle. Contains the bright star Altair, one of the vertices of the Summer Triangle.

constellations.name: Ara
constellations.description: The Altar. An ancient constellation representing a sacrificial altar.

constellations.name: Aries
constellations.description: The Ram. First constellation of the zodiac, representing the golden ram of Greek mythology.

constellations.name: Auriga
constellations.description: The Charioteer. Contains Capella, the sixth brightest star in the night sky.

constellations.name: Bootes
constellations.description: The Herdsman. Contains Arcturus, the fourth brightest star in the night sky.

constellations.name: Caelum
constellations.description: The Chisel. A small, faint constellation in the southern sky.

constellations.name: Camelopardalis
constellations.description: The Giraffe. A large but faint constellation in the northern sky.

constellations.name: Cancer
constellations.description: The Crab. A zodiac constellation, contains the Beehive Cluster (M44).

constellations.name: Canes Venatici
constellations.description: The Hunting Dogs. Contains the Whirlpool Galaxy (M51).

constellations.name: Canis Major
constellations.description: The Greater Dog. Contains Sirius, the brightest star in the night sky.

constellations.name: Canis Minor
constellations.description: The Lesser Dog. Contains Procyon, the eighth brightest star in the night sky.

constellations.name: Capricornus
constellations.description: The Sea Goat. A zodiac constellation with a distinctive arrowhead shape.

constellations.name: Carina
constellations.description: The Keel. Contains Canopus, the second brightest star in the night sky. Part of the ancient Argo Navis.

constellations.name: Cassiopeia
constellations.description: The Queen. Easily recognizable by its distinctive W shape.

constellations.name: Centaurus
constellations.description: The Centaur. Contains Alpha Centauri, the closest star system to Earth, and Omega Centauri, the brightest globular cluster.

constellations.name: Cepheus
constellations.description: The King. Named after the mythological king of Aethiopia.

constellations.name: Cetus
constellations.description: The Whale or Sea Monster. One of the largest constellations, contains the variable star Mira.

constellations.name: Chamaeleon
constellations.description: The Chameleon. A small constellation near the south celestial pole.

constellations.name: Circinus
constellations.description: The Compass. A small, faint constellation in the southern sky.

constellations.name: Columba
constellations.description: The Dove. Represents the dove that Noah sent from the ark.

constellations.name: Coma Berenices
constellations.description: Berenice's Hair. Contains the Coma Star Cluster and the North Galactic Pole.

constellations.name: Corona Australis
constellations.description: The Southern Crown. A small constellation in the southern hemisphere.

constellations.name: Corona Borealis
constellations.description: The Northern Crown. A small but distinctive semicircular constellation.

constellations.name: Corvus
constellations.description: The Crow. A small constellation with a distinctive quadrilateral shape.

constellations.name: Crater
constellations.description: The Cup. A faint constellation representing the cup of Apollo.

constellations.name: Crux
constellations.description: The Southern Cross. The smallest of all constellations but one of the most distinctive.

constellations.name: Cygnus
constellations.description: The Swan. Also known as the Northern Cross, contains the star Deneb.

constellations.name: Delphinus
constellations.description: The Dolphin. A small but distinctive constellation resembling a dolphin jumping.

constellations.name: Dorado
constellations.description: The Dolphinfish. Contains most of the Large Magellanic Cloud.

constellations.name: Draco
constellations.description: The Dragon. A large constellation winding around the north celestial pole.

constellations.name: Equuleus
constellations.description: The Little Horse. The second smallest constellation in the sky.

constellations.name: Eridanus
constellations.description: The River. The sixth largest constellation, representing a river flowing from Orion's foot.

constellations.name: Fornax
constellations.description: The Furnace. Contains the Fornax Cluster of galaxies.

constellations.name: Gemini
constellations.description: The Twins. A zodiac constellation representing Castor and Pollux.

constellations.name: Grus
constellations.description: The Crane. A constellation in the southern sky.

constellations.name: Hercules
constellations.description: The Hero. The fifth largest constellation, contains the Great Globular Cluster (M13).

constellations.name: Horologium
constellations.description: The Clock. A faint constellation in the southern sky.

constellations.name: Hydra
constellations.description: The Water Snake. The largest constellation in the sky by area.

constellations.name: Hydrus
constellations.description: The Lesser Water Snake. A small southern constellation.

constellations.name: Indus
constellations.description: The Indian. Represents a Native American.

constellations.name: Lacerta
constellations.description: The Lizard. A small, faint constellation in the northern sky.

constellations.name: Leo
constellations.description: The Lion. A zodiac constellation with a distinctive sickle shape.

constellations.name: Leo Minor
constellations.description: The Lesser Lion. A small, faint constellation between Leo and Ursa Major.

constellations.name: Lepus
constellations.description: The Hare. Located just south of Orion, representing a hare being chased by Orion.

constellations.name: Libra
constellations.description: The Scales. A zodiac constellation representing scales of justice.

constellations.name: Lupus
constellations.description: The Wolf. An ancient constellation in the southern sky.

constellations.name: Lynx
constellations.description: The Lynx. A large but faint constellation in the northern sky.

constellations.name: Lyra
constellations.description: The Lyre. Contains Vega, one of the brightest stars and part of the Summer Triangle.

constellations.name: Mensa
constellations.description: The Table Mountain. The faintest constellation, contains part of the Large Magellanic Cloud.

constellations.name: Microscopium
constellations.description: The Microscope. A small, faint constellation in the southern sky.

constellations.name: Monoceros
constellations.description: The Unicorn. Contains the Rosette Nebula.

constellations.name: Musca
constellations.description: The Fly. A small constellation in the southern sky.

constellations.name: Norma
constellations.description: The Carpenter's Square. A small, faint constellation in the southern sky.

constellations.name: Octans
constellations.description: The Octant. Contains the south celestial pole.

constellations.name: Ophiuchus
constellations.description: The Serpent Bearer. A large constellation straddling the celestial equator.

constellations.name: Orion
constellations.description: The Hunter. One of the most recognizable constellations, famous for Orion's Belt and the Orion Nebula.

constellations.name: Pavo
constellations.description: The Peacock. A constellation in the southern sky.

constellations.name: Pegasus
constellations.description: The Winged Horse. Known for the Great Square of Pegasus asterism.

constellations.name: Perseus
constellations.description: The Hero. Contains the famous variable star Algol and the Double Cluster.

constellations.name: Phoenix
constellations.description: The Phoenix. Named after the mythical bird that rises from its ashes.

constellations.name: Pictor
constellations.description: The Painter's Easel. A small, faint constellation in the southern sky.

constellations.name: Pisces
constellations.description: The Fish. A zodiac constellation representing two fish tied together.

constellations.name: Piscis Austrinus
constellations.description: The Southern Fish. Contains the bright star Fomalhaut.

constellations.name: Puppis
constellations.description: The Stern. Part of the ancient Argo Navis constellation.

constellations.name: Pyxis
constellations.description: The Compass. A small constellation representing a ship's compass.

constellations.name: Reticulum
constellations.description: The Reticle. A small constellation in the southern sky.

constellations.name: Sagitta
constellations.description: The Arrow. The third smallest constellation in the sky.

constellations.name: Sagittarius
constellations.description: The Archer. A zodiac constellation, points toward the center of the Milky Way galaxy.

constellations.name: Scorpius
constellations.description: The Scorpion. A zodiac constellation with a distinctive curved tail, contains the red supergiant Antares.

constellations.name: Sculptor
constellations.description: The Sculptor's Workshop. Contains the Sculptor Galaxy.

constellations.name: Scutum
constellations.description: The Shield. Contains the Wild Duck Cluster (M11).

constellations.name: Sextans
constellations.description: The Sextant. A faint constellation on the celestial equator.

constellations.name: Taurus
constellations.description: The Bull. A zodiac constellation containing the Pleiades star cluster and the red giant Aldebaran.

constellations.name: Telescopium
constellations.description: The Telescope. A small constellation in the southern sky.

constellations.name: Triangulum
constellations.description: The Triangle. Contains the Triangulum Galaxy (M33).

constellations.name: Triangulum Australe
constellations.description: The Southern Triangle. A small but bright constellation in the southern sky.

constellations.name: Tucana
constellations.description: The Toucan. Contains the Small Magellanic Cloud and the globular cluster 47 Tucanae.

constellations.name: Ursa Major
constellations.description: The Great Bear. Contains the Big Dipper asterism, one of the most recognizable star patterns.

constellations.name: Ursa Minor
constellations.description: The Little Bear. Contains Polaris, the North Star, at the end of its tail.

constellations.name: Vela
constellations.description: The Sails. Part of the ancient Argo Navis, contains the Vela Supernova Remnant.

constellations.name: Virgo
constellations.description: The Maiden. A zodiac constellation containing Spica and the Virgo Cluster of galaxies.

constellations.name: Volans
constellations.description: The Flying Fish. A small constellation in the southern sky.

constellations.name: Vulpecula
constellations.description: The Fox. Contains the Dumbbell Nebula (M27).
